title: The Tenant Who Never Existed
narration:
Do not buy a house until you check behind the mirror. I just found out I’m not the only one living here.

I pulled back the master bedroom glass. Behind it sat a hidden cabinet packed with decades of Polaroids.

Each photo shows the same man in my living room. The wallpaper changes, the furniture shifts, but he never ages.

The final photo shows him holding a modern iPhone. The film is yellowed, yet the screen light is blindingly bright.

I checked the blueprints, but this room was never built.
